source_pr.title: planner,variable: disable tidb_enable_reuse_chunk when reading json/text column
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #41366

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Queries reading JSON/TEXT/BLOB (or very long VARCHAR) columns can behave unstably when chunk reuse is enabled; tests observe unexpected `@@last_sql_use_alloc` or wrong results due to reused backing buffers.
failure_signature: executor tests: `@@last_sql_use_alloc` expected 0 for long varlen columns but got 1 (chunk reuse enabled), risking incorrect results due to buffer reuse
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness was caused by unsafe reuse of chunk memory (mutable backing buffer aliasing): when `tidb_enable_reuse_chunk` is on, reading long varlen types (JSON/TEXT/BLOB or very long VARCHAR) can lead to values being backed by reused buffers and becoming incorrect/unstable. The fix makes the optimizer detect plans that read such types and disables chunk reuse by clearing the allocator, and adds/updates regression tests that assert `@@last_sql_use_alloc=0` for these cases while keeping reuse for safe column types.
fix_pattern: Detect long varlen column reads in the physical plan and disable chunk reuse (clear allocator); add regression tests asserting `@@last_sql_use_alloc` and stable results.

evidence: files: executor/executor_test.go, executor/explain_test.go, executor/index_lookup_join_test.go, executor/simpletest/BUILD.bazel, executor/simpletest/chunk_reuse_test.go, planner/core/optimizer.go, sessionctx/variable/session.go
